text: The entire single-stage AFB VATS procedure was performed in a hybrid operating room which equipped with robotic C-arm cone beam CT system.
After anesthetic induction, the patient was intubated with a single lumen tube for ventilation.
After general anesthesia, the patient was placed in lateral position with the operated site up and fixed with tapes properly.
The ventilation was hold in an end-inspiratory phase by clamping the tracheal tube with a hemostat.
A 5-second Dyna-CT scan was made during the inspiration holding, and the lesion side was covered in a scan.
When doing AFB procedure under general anesthesia, the bronchoscope can be inserted through the endotracheal tube
Using ultra-thin bronchoscope, more distal bronchial orifices can be accessed before choosing one of them for marking.
Once identifying the nearest bronchi that could lead to the target lesion, the four French straight-tip catheter was inserted into the selected bronchial orifice.
Under the augmented fluoroscopy, the discrepancy between the target lesion and the tip of the catheter can be real-timely assessed.
The CRM was rotated to different angles for checking the exact position of the catheter tip, and the contrast dye was injected to several spots on the pleura surface, which could surround the target.
A bronchial block was inserted into the endotracheal tube for one-lung ventilation.
The patient was placed in the standard lateral decubitus position, with upper arm support and lateral flexion.
The operation site was sterilized and draped for thoracoscopic surgery.
During the surgery, the thoracoscopic findings should match up with the post-marking 3D image, and the resection can be performed according to the surgical plan made on the 3D image.
That ensured the resection of the target lesion.
For the deep-seated lesions, microcoil was placed transbronchially using the catheter and guidewire.
During the surgery, the microcoil can be visualized under the intraoperative fluoroscopy, and the inclusion of the microcoil by the stapler can secure the adequate resection margin.
